sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content:
World model (revised): Clicking on the red center of the bottom-right panel's center cell (46,46) did NOT change the board. The step counter incremented but no gameplay objects changed. I need to try other click locations to understand the interaction mechanic.
Goal model: Still need to figure out what clicking does. The 4 panels show different 3x3 grid patterns, and the bottom-right is framed as the target.
Plan: Try clicking on different cells in the panels to find what interaction works. Let me also check if the orange bar at the bottom changed.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check what changed between frames
# Compare previous_frame to current_frame
if previous_frame:
    prev_seg = previous_frame.segmentation
    curr_seg = current_frame.segmentation
    
    prev_nodes = {n['id']: n for n in prev_seg['nodes']}
    curr_nodes = {n['id']: n for n in curr_seg['nodes']}
    
    # Check if any nodes changed
    changes = []
    for nid in curr_nodes:
        if nid in prev_nodes:
            pn = prev_nodes[nid]
            cn = curr_nodes[nid]
            if pn['hash'] != cn['hash']:
                changes.append(f"Node {nid}: hash changed")
            # Check boundary
            if pn['boundary'] != cn['boundary']:
                changes.append(f"Node {nid}: boundary changed")
        else:
            changes.append(f"Node {nid}: new")
    
    for nid in prev_nodes:
        if nid not in curr_nodes:
            changes.append(f"Node {nid}: removed")
    
    if changes:
        print("Changes detected:", changes)
    else:
        print("No changes detected in nodes")
    
    # Check step
    print(f"Previous step: {previous_frame.step}, Current step: {current_frame.step}")
else:
    print("No previous frame")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
